userMessage: I2C signal integrity test: CH1 SCL 3.3V DC 1Mohm, CH2 SDA 3.3V DC 1Mohm. Set up I2C decode B1 clock CH1 1.65V data CH2 1.65V. Add setup time and hold time measurements. Trigger on I2C address 0x48 write. Single sequence. Query setup and hold results. Save both channels binary. Screenshot.

response.text:
I can’t emit applyable TekAutomate JSON safely for this request.

Verified SCPI was sufficient for:
- channel termination: `CH<x>:TERmination`
- I2C decode sources/thresholds:
  - `BUS:B<x>:I2C:CLOCk:SOUrce`
  - `BUS:B<x>:I2C:CLOCk:THReshold`
  - `BUS:B<x>:I2C:DATa:SOUrce`
  - `BUS:B<x>:I2C:DATa:THReshold`
- I2C trigger pieces:
  - `TRIGger:{A|B}:TYPe`
  - `TRIGger:{A|B}:BUS:B<x>:I2C:CONDition`
  - `TRIGger:{A|B}:BUS:B<x>:I2C:ADDRess:MODe`
  - `TRIGger:{A|B}:BUS:B<x>:I2C:ADDRess:VALue`
  - `TRIGger:{A|B}:BUS:B<x>:I2C:DATa:DIRection`
- single sequence:
  - `ACQuire:STOPAfter`
  - `ACQuire:STATE`
- measurement creation/results:
  - `MEASUrement:ADDMEAS`
  - `MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:TYPe`
  - `MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:RESUlts:CURRentacq:MEAN?`

But exact verification was not sufficient for the full requested flow because these required pieces were not verified in the retrieved results:
- explicit command to set bus B1 protocol/type to I2C
- exact source assignment commands for `SETUP` and `HOLD` measurements on MSO4/5/6
  - retrieved `MEASUrement:MEAS<x>:SOURCE` is not enough to safely prove two-source setup/hold measurement wiring for CH1/CH2

So I’d be guessing on the measurement configuration, which the policy forbids.
